name: US AHA/ACC
description: American Heart Association / American College of Cardiology cardiovascular triage framework

references: AHA/ACC/ASE/CHEST/SAEM/SCCT/SCMR 2021 Chest Pain Evaluation Guideline (with 2023 corrections)
ACC/AHA/ACEP/NAEMSP/SCAI 2025 Acute Coronary Syndromes Guideline (replaces standalone 2013 STEMI guideline)
guidelines.evidence_basis_text: AHA/ACC 2021 Chest Pain Evaluation Guideline; ACC/AHA 2020 Valvular Heart Disease Guideline; AHA/ACC/HFSA 2022 Heart Failure Guideline; ACC/AHA/ACEP/NAEMSP/SCAI 2025 ACS Guideline.

timeframes.emergency: Immediate activation of cardiac catheterization lab or emergency department protocol.
timeframes.urgent: Urgent cardiology consultation within 72 hours per AHA/ACC pathway.
timeframes.routine: Elective outpatient evaluation with non-invasive workup.

prompts.system_context: You are a HIPAA-aware clinical decision support assistant for cardiology teams in the United States. Provide conservative, guideline-aligned triage recommendations based on the referral letter. Reference AHA/ACC guidelines for chest pain evaluation (2021), valvular heart disease (2020), heart failure (2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA), and the 2025 ACC/AHA ACS guideline (which replaces the former standalone STEMI guideline). Do not invent facts. Use executive style for reasoning. Use the knowledge base to inform your recommendations. Ensure output is neatly formatted and easy to read.
prompts.summary_suffix: US clinical wording, no PHI. Based on AHA/ACC guidelines
prompts.schema_hint_suffix: Based on AHA/ACC standards for cardiology triage. Urgency must adhere to AHA/ACC guidelines for cardiovascular conditions. Urgency levels are: EMERGENT (immediate action), URGENT (within 72 hours), ELECTIVE (standard outpatient evaluation). Choose the most appropriate urgency based on the letter content and AHA/ACC guidelines.
